qa_pairs.question: How many Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs) on an H100 SXM5 GPU are not assigned to any instance when Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology is used?
qa_pairs.answer: When an H100 SXM5 GPU is partitioned using Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology, eight Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs) remain unassigned and are effectively lost from the total of 132 SMs.

qa_pairs.question: According to the document, why is it more accurate to describe the partitioning of an H100 GPU under Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology in 'thirty-thirdths' rather than 'eighths'?
qa_pairs.answer: The document suggests considering a MIGed H100 GPU as divided into 'thirty-thirdths' because the H100 SXM5 GPU has 132 Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs), which do not partition cleanly into eighths. For example, what NVidia refers to as 'one eighth' actually corresponds to 4/33 of an H100 GPU's processing units.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Streaming Multiprocessor (SM) allocation for an `nvidia_h100_80gb_hbm3_1g.10gb` instance when an H100 SXM5 GPU is configured with Multi-Instance GPU (MIG)?
qa_pairs.answer: When an H100 SXM5 GPU is configured with Multi-Instance GPU (MIG), each `nvidia_h100_80gb_hbm3_1g.10gb` instance is allocated 16 Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs), and two such instances are created.
